title: Wang2016 – SSVEP Wang 2016 dataset
description: A benchmark steady-state visual evoked potential (SSVEP) dataset comprising EEG recordings from 34 healthy subjects performing a 40-target brain-computer interface speller task. Subjects viewed flickering visual stimuli at frequencies ranging from 8 to 15.8 Hz with 0.2 Hz resolution, using a joint frequency and phase modulation approach. The dataset includes 64-channel EEG data sampled at 250 Hz with 6 trials per target frequency, providing a comprehensive resource for developing and validating SSVEP-based BCI algorithms.
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Synamps2 system (Neuroscan, Inc.) at 1000 Hz sampling rate with 64 channels positioned according to the international 10-20 system, referenced to vertex (Cz). Online filters included a bandpass filter (0.15-200 Hz) and 50 Hz notch filter. Electrode impedances were maintained below 10 kΩ. Continuous EEG was segmented into 6-second epochs (0.5 s pre-stimulus, 5.5 s post-stimulus) and downsampled to 250 Hz. Subjects performed a cue-guided target selection task with 40 flickering characters presented in a 5×8 matrix on a 23.6-inch LCD monitor at 60 Hz refresh rate, viewed from 70 cm distance.
